attacks.name: Hypno Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, this attack does 30 damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sacred Wing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack's base damage is 20 instead of 60.

attacks.name: Jump Over
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them. Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Leaf Slice
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Roll Over
attacks.effect: Snorlax is now Asleep. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: So rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts. Only a few people have seen it worldwide.

attacks.name: Lightning Rod
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and put a Lightning Rod marker on it. (A Pokémon can have only 1 Lightning Rod marker on it at a time.)

attacks.name: Lightning Bolt
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to any Pokémon with a Lightning Rod marker on it. Apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: Normally found near power plants, it can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.effect: Scyther can't use this attack during your next turn. (Benching or evolving Scyther ends this effect.)

flavorText: With ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one.

attacks.name: Burning Fire
attacks.effect: When you use this attack, you may discard any number of Fire Energy cards attached to your Pokémon in play. This attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Fire Energy card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Its body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly among flames.

attacks.name: Seething Anger
attacks.effect: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of damage counters on Machamp. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

flavorText: Using its amazing muscles, it throws powerful punches that can knock its victim clear over the horizon.

fullDisplayName: Pokémon Tower 42 Wizards Black Star Promos
rules:
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

If the effect of a Pokémon Power, attack, Energy card, or Trainer card would put a card in a discard pile into its owner's hand, that card stays in that discard pile instead.

fullDisplayName: Lucky Stadium 41 Wizards Black Star Promos
rules:
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.

Once during each player's turn (before attacking), that player may flip a coin. If heads, that player draws a card.

fullDisplayName: Pokémon Center 40 Wizards Black Star Promos
rules: Remove all damage counters from all of your own Pokémon with damage counters on them, then discard all Energy cards attached to those Pokémon.

attacks.name: Pain Split
attacks.effect: Put 1 damage counter on the Defending Pokémon for each damage counter on Misdreavus.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It loves to bite and yank people's hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.
flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

attacks.name: Repeating Kick
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rapid Spin
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with his or her Active Pokémon, then, if you have any Benched Pokémon, you switch 1 of them with your Active Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

flavorText: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

attacks.name: Good Night Song
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

rules: If this Baby Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.
flavorText: Its extremely flexible and elastic body makes it bounce continuously—anytime, anywhere.

attacks.name: Let's Play!
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Baby Pokémon card and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

rules: If this Baby Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.
flavorText: It is not yet skilled at storing electricity. It may send out a jolt if amused or startled.

attacks.name: Protective Flame
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Volcanoes erupt when it barks. Unable to restrain its extreme power, it races headlong around the land.

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Scizor during your opponent's next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Metal Pincer
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its wings are not used for flying. They are flapped at high speed to adjust its body temperature.

attacks.name: Paint
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a type (other than Colorless) and put a Coloring counter on the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now the type you chose. If it already had a Coloring counter, remove the old one. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Once it becomes an adult, it has a tendency to let its comrades plant footprints on its back.

attacks.name: Eek
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

rules: If this Baby Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.
flavorText: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

attacks.name: Snivel
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Togepi during your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Togepi is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Mini-Metronome
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Mini-Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else in order to use that attack.) (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Togepi's type is still Colorless.) Togepi performs that attack.

flavorText: Still only a hatchling, it uses poison to chase off its enemies when threatened.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Marill but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: The tip of its tail, which contains oil that is lighter than water, lets it swim without drowning.

fullDisplayName: Surfing Pikachu 28 Wizards Black Star Promos
flavorText: One summer, a group of Pikachus was found riding the waves at the local beach.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Pikachu.

flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can cause lightning storms.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy cards attached to Pikachu.

flavorText: It uses its sensitive tail to sense its environment and has been known to react violently if its tail is grabbed.

fullDisplayName: Flying Pikachu 25 Wizards Black Star Promos

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Flying Pikachu; if tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

flavorText: By learning how to fly, Pikachu overcame its weakness to Fighting Pokémon.

attacks.name: Birthday Surprise
attacks.effect: If it's not your birthday, this attack does 30 damage. If it is your birthday, flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 50 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage.

attacks.name: Lightning Burn
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to be present wherever there is a lightning storm.

attacks.name: Diamond Dust
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to freeze the water in the air during winter, causing snow.

attacks.name: Hyper Flame
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Moltres. If tails, discard all Energy cards attached to Moltres. If you can't discard Energy cards, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The flames on this legendary Pokémon's wings burn so brightly that they can make night seem like day.

attacks.name: Headache
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play Trainer cards during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: While lulling its enemies with its vacant look, this wily Pokémon will use psychokinetic powers.

fullDisplayName: Sabrina's Abra 19 Wizards Black Star Promos

attacks.name: Synchronize
attacks.effect: This attack can't be used unless Sabrina's Abra and the Defending Pokémon have the same number of Energy cards attached to them.

fullDisplayName: Team Rocket's Meowth 18 Wizards Black Star Promos

attacks.name: Miraculous Comeback
attacks.effect: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Pokémon in play. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads. Then Team Rocket's Meowth does 10 damage times the number of tails to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon is more active at night. It likes bright, shiny things and cannot resist taking them and adding them to its collection.

fullDisplayName: Dark Persian 17 Wizards Black Star Promos

attacks.name: Tempt
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. if heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Popular with women because of its beautiful fur. The leader of the Rockets keeps one as a pet.

fullDisplayName: Computer Error 16 Wizards Black Star Promos
rules: You may draw up to 5 cards, then your opponent may draw up to 5 cards. Your turn is over now (you don't get to attack).

fullDisplayName: Cool Porygon 15 Wizards Black Star Promos

attacks.name: Texture Magic
attacks.effect: You may change Cool Porygon's Resistance to a type of your choice other than Colorless. If the Defending Pokémon has a Weakness, you may change it to a type of your choice other than Colorless. (Benching either Pokémon ends the effect on that Pokémon.)

attacks.name: 3-D Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A Pokémon that consists entirely of programming code. Capable of moving freely in cyberspace.

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.effect: Choose up to 2 Energy cards from your discard pile and attach them to Mewtwo.

flavorText: Years of genetic experiments resulted in the creation of this never-before-seen violent Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Remove a number of damage counters from Venusaur equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Venusaur has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

attacks.name: Energy Control
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and attach it to another of your opponent's Pokémon of your choice.

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.
Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from elemental stones.

attacks.name: Cat Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage. If tails and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Devolution Beam
attacks.effect: Choose an evolved Pokémon (your own or your opponent's). Return the highest Stage Evolution card on that Pokémon to its player's hand. That Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned, or anything else that might be the result of an attack (just as if you had evolved it).

flavorText: So rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts. Only a few people have seen it worldwide.

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Devolution Beam
attacks.effect: Choose an evolved Pokémon (your own or your opponent's). Return the highest Stage Evolution card on that Pokémon to its player's hand. That Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned, or anything else that might be the result of an attack (just as if you had evolved it).

flavorText: So rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts. Only a few people have seen it worldwide.

attacks.name: First Aid
attacks.effect: Remove 1 damage counter from Jigglypuff.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.effect: Jigglypuff does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: When its huge eyes light up, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Flames of Rage
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Fire Energy cards attached to Arcanine in order to use this attack. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Arcanine.

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon famous for its beauty. It looks almost as if it flies when it runs.

attacks.name: Supersonic Flight
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon can fly is spite of its large bulk. It is said to be able to circumnavigate the earth in just 16 hours.

attacks.name: Recharge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Pikachu. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy cards attached to Pikachu in order to use this attack.

flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, they attract so much electricity that they can cause lightning to strike.

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.effect: Choose up to 2 Energy cards from your discard pile and attach them to Mewtwo.

flavorText: Years of genetic experiments resulted in the creation of this never-before-seen violent Pokémon.

attacks.name: Light Screen
attacks.effect: Whenever an attack does damage to Electabuzz (after applying Weakness and Resistance) during your opponent's next turn, that attack only does half the damage to Electabuzz (rounded down to the nearest 10). (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: A wild Pokémon with a short temper. It is able to distinguish colors, and likes the color red.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Pikachu during your opponent's next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity could build and cause lightning storms.
